steps.expected: Prompt text instructs model to return compressed_details key
steps.observed: Line 178: prompt includes '- "compressed_details": comma-separated list of specifics you omitted from the fact (e.g. "exact config values, error message text, specific file paths"). If nothing was omitted, use "none".'

steps.expected: compressed_details is part of the JSON schema instructions
steps.observed: The prompt says 'Return a JSON array of objects with these keys:' followed by fact, category, and compressed_details with description

steps.expected: Parsing loop reads compressed_details from LLM response and includes in returned dicts
steps.observed: Line 199: 'compressed_details': f.get('compressed_details', '')[:500] — extracted from each LLM response object with default empty string and truncated to 500 chars

steps.expected: extract_facts_llm accepts existing_facts parameter and includes in prompt
steps.observed: Line 120: function signature includes 'existing_facts=None'. Lines 148-153: if existing_facts, builds 'ALREADY KNOWN FACTS (do NOT re-extract these or minor variations of them):' section with bullet list of facts, followed by 'Only extract facts that are GENUINELY NEW information not covered above.'

assertions.title: Existing facts loaded before session loop

steps.expected: Loads existing facts (confidence > 0, limit 50) before the per-session loop and passes them to extract_facts_llm()
steps.observed: Lines 486-489: 'existing_facts_rows = conn.execute("SELECT fact FROM facts WHERE confidence > 0 ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 50").fetchall()' and 'existing_facts = [r[0] for r in existing_facts_rows]' — happens BEFORE the for-loop at line 494. Line 509: passed as 'existing_facts=existing_facts' to extract_facts_llm()

steps.expected: Level 1: normal (temp 0.1, timeout 180s). Level 2: conservative (temp 0.05, timeout 90s). Level 3: return empty (heuristic fallback).
steps.observed: Line 208-209: '# Three-level retry: normal → conservative → heuristic fallback' and '# Level 1: Normal attempt (temp 0.1, timeout 180s)'. Lines 216,218: temperature=0.1, timeout=180. Line 231: '# Level 2: Retry with lower temperature and shorter timeout'. Lines 238,240: temperature=0.05, timeout=90. Line 253: '# Level 3: Return empty — caller runs heuristic extraction separately' followed by 'return []'.

steps.action: Inspect stderr print statements in retry blocks (lines 224-250)
steps.expected: Each retry level prints a message to stderr with failure reason and escalation level
steps.observed: Lines 226-228: Level 1 failure prints 'LLM extraction: normal attempt failed ({reason}), retrying with conservative settings...' to file=sys.stderr. Lines 248-250: Level 2 failure prints 'LLM extraction: retry also failed ({reason}), falling back to heuristic only' to file=sys.stderr. Both include the exception reason string via f-string interpolation.

steps.action: grep -n 'INSERT INTO facts' src/distill.py and inspect store_facts function
steps.expected: INSERT statement includes compressed_details column and value
steps.observed: Lines 356-361: INSERT INTO facts includes 'compressed_details' in column list (line 357) and f.get('compressed_details', '') as the 8th parameter value (line 361).

steps.expected: model parameter defaults to 'llama3.3:70b' and is used in API call body via variable (not hardcoded separately)
steps.observed: Line 119: extract_facts_llm signature has 'model: str = "llama3.3:70b"'. Line 214: Level 1 API call uses '"model": model' (variable reference). Line 236: Level 2 retry also uses '"model": model' (variable reference). Line 473: distill() also defaults to 'model: str = "llama3.3:70b"'. Line 534: argparse default is also 'llama3.3:70b'. The model variable is consistently threaded through — never hardcoded in the API call body.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. All distill.py features verified via code inspection — compressed_details in prompt/parsing/storage, existing_facts dedup context, three-level retry with stderr logging, and correct model parameter defaults.
